channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cruise ship cabin steward demonstrates how decorative towel animals are created for hotel and cruise guests
item.description: The article explores the increasingly popular practice of towel art in hotels and cruise ships, where cabin stewards transform ordinary towels into decorative shapes such as swans, frogs, dogs, crocod...

item.title: Letters to the editor: Perspectives on Alberta separatism debate, national unity, energy policy and Canadian municipal politics
item.description: This collection of letters to the editor reflects a range of Canadian public opinion on current political, energy, and cultural issues. One contributor comments on Alberta Premier Danielle Smith’s app...

item.title: Questions raised over parliamentary oversight of the Department of Parliamentary Services
item.description: The article examines the limited public and parliamentary scrutiny applied to the Department of Parliamentary Services (DPS), the body responsible for providing operational, administrative and support...

item.title: IPL rules clarify eligibility of late injury replacements for retention ahead of auctions
item.description: The Indian Premier League (IPL) has clarified an important rule regarding injury replacements and their eligibility for retention ahead of future auctions. According to the IPL’s regulations, if a pla...

item.title: A Retrospective on Rose Valois and the Golden Age of Parisian Hat Design
item.description: The article explores the legacy of Rose Valois, one of the most influential French millinery houses of the mid-20th century. Founded in Paris in 1927 by Fernande Léger, Germaine de Valois, and Rose Ma...

item.title: Mark Seliger’s 1999 Rolling Stone Portraits Presented Brad Pitt in a Surreal and Unconventional Style
item.description: The article revisits a memorable 1999 photo session in which photographer Mark Seliger captured actor Brad Pitt for Rolling Stone magazine. The portraits stand out because they departed from the polis...

item.title: Australian activists freed after Gaza aid flotilla detention allege mistreatment in Israeli custody
item.description: Several Australians who took part in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla have returned home after being detained by Israeli authorities in international waters west of Cyprus. Eleven Australians were among arou...

item.title: How Krack Cream Built a Market Leader by Solving the Overlooked Problem of Cracked Heels
item.description: This article presents a business case study on Krack Cream, a skincare product developed by Paras Pharmaceuticals that successfully identified and addressed an overlooked yet widespread problem: crack...

item.title: Sophie Thompson performs Wendy Cope poem to honour independent greengrocers in Celebration Day short film
item.description: In a short film produced for The Independent’s Independent TV platform, actor Sophie Thompson performs a reading of Wendy Cope’s poem ‘The Orange’ as a tribute to three independent greengrocers who ha...

item.title: Northwest families pool resources as livestock prices surge ahead of Eid-el-Kabir
item.description: As Eid-el-Kabir dey approach with less than a week remaining, livestock prices across Northwest Nigeria don sharply rise, pushing many Muslim families to rethink how dem go afford sacrificial animals....

item.title: Coal mine gas explosion in China kills at least 82, injures over 120
item.description: A devastating gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in Qinyuan County, Shanxi province, China, has left at least 82 people dead and more than 120 others hospitalized, according to reports from the ...

item.title: Supreme Court transfers Twisha Sharma death probe to CBI, urges media restraint amid ongoing investigation
item.description: The Supreme Court of India took suo motu cognisance of the death of former model and actor Twisha Sharma, who was found dead at her matrimonial home in Bhopal on May 12. During the hearing, a bench le...

item.title: Japan begins geological review of Minamitorishima as potential site for nuclear waste disposal
item.description: Japan’s National Waste Management Organization (NUMO) has initiated the first stage of an assessment to determine whether Minamitorishima, a remote island in the Ogasawara chain, could serve as a long...

item.title: Obituary: Pierrette Lucas, Canadian diplomat and public servant remembered for her independent spirit
item.description: Pierrette Lucas, born Pierrette Alain in Montreal on November 28, 1940, and died on March 5, 2026 in Victoria at age 85, is remembered as a charismatic, unconventional, and influential Canadian public...

item.title: Sizwe Dhlomo responds to reports of Unathi Nkayi’s alleged drunk driving arrest
item.description: Radio personality Sizwe Dhlomo has publicly reacted to reports that former broadcaster Unathi Nkayi was allegedly arrested for drunk driving. His reaction came after he reshared a Sunday Times post on...

item.title: Melrose Arch records low vacancy rates and rising tenant demand in Johannesburg
item.description: Melrose Arch continues to strengthen its position as one of Johannesburg’s leading mixed-use commercial precincts, supported by strong tenant demand, low vacancy levels and ongoing retail and hospital...

item.title: How political mythmaking shapes perceptions of the drug war and accountability in the Philippines
item.description: The opinion piece examines how political mythmaking is used by Filipino leaders to shape public perception, focusing on Senator Alan Peter Cayetano’s rhetoric and its implications. It explains that po...

item.title: Three Canadian flotilla activists return to Vancouver after detention in Israel
item.description: Three Canadian activists who had been detained in Israel after participating in a flotilla have returned safely to Vancouver, according to reporting by The Canadian Press. The individuals arrived in t...

item.title: John Laws bequeaths $5.5 million art collection to the Art Gallery of NSW
item.description: The late Australian radio broadcaster John Laws has made a significant cultural contribution through the quiet donation of 10 artworks valued at approximately $5.5 million to the Art Gallery of New So...

item.title: US and Iran continue slow negotiations over Strait of Hormuz and nuclear program
item.description: US President Donald Trump has stated that his administration will not rush negotiations with Iran over a possible peace agreement aimed at ending the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Although Trum...
